mūla.devanāgarī: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु | मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे
mūla.iast: manmanā bhava madbhakto madyājī māṃ namaskuru | māmevaiṣyasi satyaṃ te pratijāne priyo'si me
mūla.chapter_position: Chapter 18 (Mokṣa-Sannyāsa-Yoga (The Yoga of Liberation by Renunciation)), verse 65
mūla.speaker: Krishna

word_by_word.grammar: 2nd person singular simple future active verb
1st person singular present indicative active verb

senses_attested_in_panel.sense: ? सत्यां प्रतिज्ञां करोमि एतस्मिन् वस्तुनि इत्यर्थः यतः प्रियः असि मे
तव प्रतिज्ञां करोमि? न उपच्छन्दमात्रं यतः त्वं प्रियः असि मे प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स

corpus_provenance.mūla: Belvalkar critical edition (BORI 1947), via Ambuda multi-witness

so_what_questions: If Krsna promises 'you will reach Me' only to Arjuna because 'you are dear to Me' — is this a universal promise or a personal one, and what does that mean for practitioners who are not Arjuna?

The fourfold injunction (mind, devotion, worship, prostration) is given without specifying the quality or quantity required — is the promise conditional on a threshold of practice, or is the orientation itself sufficient?

Shankara reads this as preparing for samyag-darshana (right vision); Ramanuja as arriving at Vaikuntha — are these descriptions of the same attainment or genuinely different soteriological destinations?
Madhusudana distinguishes love-based from hatred-based fixation on Krsna — what does this imply about the relationship between motivation and result in spiritual practice generally?
If the promise is 'true' (satyam) and grounded in mutual priti (love), does this make Krsna's grace a responsive mechanism (love calls forth love) or a sovereign act (grace precedes merit)?
Vallabha insists on exclusive devotion ('eva' — alone): how does this relate to the preceding chapters' pluralism about paths (karma, jñana, bhakti)? Is 18.65 a supersession or a synthesis?
The verse occurs in the 'paramaguhya' (supreme secret) section of chapter 18: what makes this instruction more secret than the earlier teaching on bhakti in chapter 12?

everyday_applications.advaita: Before beginning any significant task today, pause and explicitly surrender both the goal and the means to Vasudeva — not as ritual but as a recognition that you are not the owner of either. Notice how this shifts the quality of attention during the task itself.
everyday_applications.viśiṣṭādvaita: Maintain an unbroken thread of remembrance (smriti-santana) of the Lord throughout your day — not prayer at fixed times, but a sustained background awareness, the way a musician holds the shruti (drone) beneath the melody of daily activity.
everyday_applications.dvaita: At moments of achievement or failure today, practice recognizing that both your capacity and the outcome belong to Hari as the independent reality (svatantra). Your role is to act as a perfectly transparent instrument, with no residue of 'I did this.'
everyday_applications.śuddhādvaita: Choose one relationship or responsibility today and bring your full, undivided attention to it — no parallel tracking, no split loyalty. This 'eva' (exclusivity) applied to a single hour is Vallabha's pushti-marga made concrete and portable.
everyday_applications.bhakti: When you feel doubt about whether your practice is 'working,' return to Sridhara's frame: the promise is not conditional on visible progress but on the Lord's prasada (grace). Your part is to remain oriented; the knowledge will come through the Lord's mercy, not through your measurement.
everyday_applications.advaita-bhakti: Examine whether your attention to a person, idea, or goal you are 'devoted to' today is prema-yukta (love-informed) or dwesa-yukta (anxiety-driven). Madhusudana's distinction: both can generate obsessive focus, but only one qualifies as bhakti — what is the texture of your attention?
